headline: THE NAMES THAT HOLD YOUR LICENCE
subheading: How the QP, RP, RPi and GMP heads make or break your MIA or WDA.
flux_prompt: An abstract editorial conceptual composition representing accountable named individuals upholding a UK pharmaceutical manufacturing and wholesale licence. A central glowing official authorisation document or seal rendered in deep navy and teal, suspended and supported from below by four slender luminous cyan pillars, each topped with a softly glowing minimalist human silhouette badge or lanyard medallion, symbolising the QP, RP, RPi and the heads of production and quality control. Fine interconnecting light lines and a subtle organisational chart lattice link the figures to the document, evoking authority, separation of duties and a chain of personal responsibility. Floating translucent geometric panels suggest batch records, distribution routes and quality data flowing between the roles. A delicate molecular helix motif and faint vial silhouettes drift in the deeply blurred background. Premium clinical-luxury aesthetic, immaculate glass and frosted surfaces, deep navy base with teal and cyan light, a single fresh-green accent glow highlighting the central seal, shallow depth of field, polished and authoritative. 1500x900 composition, 5:3 aspect ratio, professional editorial illustration, premium quality, clean composition, modern visual storytelling, professional lighting, no text, no watermark, no logo, ultra detailed
